guest: Oliver Jay, Jordan Van Horn, Danny Herzberg, Zhenya Logunov, Lauren Schwartz, Carl Parrish
guest_role: Multiple sales leaders compilation: OJ (former CRO, Asana), Jordan Van Horn (revenue leader, Monte Carlo), Danny Herzberg (partner, Sequoia; ex-Slack/HubSpot), Zhenya Logunov (CRO, Miro), Lauren Schwartz (VP Enterprise Sales, 5Chrome), Carl Parrish (VP Sales, Figma)
assertiveness_why: This is the '20 Sales' compilation format with short, clarifying, mostly receptive questions ('just a dumb question', 'just pausing that') and light role-play hypotheticals, with no real contesting of any guest's claims.
question_style: Mostly short clarifying and sequencing questions ('so we put them with product for a week?', 'when do we let them speak to customers?') designed to extract a structured playbook; Harry occasionally role-plays as a founder asking for guidance but does not challenge or push back on any answer.
tone: Upbeat, efficient, and appreciative host-of-a-clinic tone; treats each guest as an expert to be mined for tactics rather than a subject to interrogate.
depth: High tactical/operational depth on sales onboarding specifically: staged onboarding (product/support to market to sales), ramp timelines, red-flag detection in first 30-90 days, PLG vs enterprise onboarding differences, deal-review cadences.
harry_quotes: Just so I get that right, so we put them with product, they sit with product for a week, a day?
Should I expect you to do it or should I give you the guidance?
What are the biggest signs in the first three months that I've fucked up this hire?
learnings: Early-stage onboarding should sequence product/support knowledge, then market context, then sales training last, since sales-specific habits are the hardest to unlearn once formed.
New enterprise reps should get low-risk customer exposure first (recorded calls, low-stakes accounts) before being trusted with major logos.
The clearest 30-90 day success signal is demonstrated impact, not activity metrics like pipeline coverage or a well-written book of business.
New sales leaders should spend their first month on a customer listening tour rather than internal strategy work, to build customer-oriented credibility.
PLG company reps must still be proactive (manufacturing outbound, synthesizing user interviews for outreach) rather than waiting passively for product-qualified leads.
notable: This is a multi-guest compilation episode (20 Sales format) stitching together short segments from six different sales leaders rather than one continuous interview.
